transcript.clean_text: yet another remarkable day on la vuette well spain is absolutely alive with enthusiasm for the race after two spanish winners harada joining solaire on the roster well after stretching their sinews in the run into sistana where the breakaway had success how would it pan out on the big mountain day for this man surely he was going to be attacked lead of just 21 seconds over rudy mollar of group arma francis de gea and mcmas 28 seconds margin there was no doubt that ever nepal's quick step alpha vinyl would be under pressure how would they sustain it a quick look at the course suggested that this day may well turn out to be two races in one a breakaway and indeed a gc battle they could also all come together as we did for the birthday celebrations of marco brenna sadly for his team eco aunt and later mark donovan both testing positive for covid and having to go home the red jersey ready for action and so were his team what on earth could roglic jumbo visma and india's grenadiers do about it but one look at the course was enough to frighten anybody so many climbs along the way and a category one test at the very end drama guaranteed and they were climbing don't forget straight out of the back door well it took a while for our breakaway to finally be established the first climb over the top jay vine on the attack sounds familiar well he and mark solaire winners of stages five and six teamed up and really they were asking a question of the entire pelican finally we had a ten-man breakaway up the road and it was high quality grammar francis is just tebow pinot had to work hard he and sebastian reichenbach while they were able to get onto the back wheel of bruno amirai who dropped back from further up the road and installed three teammates in the front group while the gap was significant it moved up to around about four minutes quick step alpha vinyl did not panic they were trying to preserve some resources at least for the climbs but the pace was high and each one of those climbs seemed to fall to jay vine if he couldn't win another stage then at least he wanted to win a pokemon jersey his gap in that competition became unbridgeable but what about the gap to the break another man within it mass pedestal spectacularly so he's there in the sprints he's also there in the climbs and he was there at the intermediate sprint and went to degree on the final climb all hell was breaking loose amongst the pre-race gc favorites junior philippe did his turn as dutifully as he always does for romico anova nepal who was burning up his teammates meanwhile jay vine was burning off anyone who was in the break tebow pinot desperate to stay with him by tara may likewise for the likes of lander were gone well with five kilometers remaining it seemed certain that vine would make it all the way but a final question still had to be asked out there by mark and flowed between the pair of them vine still looking good and so was remko evernote the names he was putting to the sword in his wake were significant this was as big a statement as was being made up front staying with him henrik mass primos roglic it was all getting very selective further back down the mountain nicolanga lopez it seems his legs had woken up and the world is waking up to jaivene a phenomenon mark solaire also a stage winner closed the gap down to around 25 seconds it then widened once more the sun came out this time his first stage win was shrouded in cloud they parted for him today magnificent jay vine wins again mark solaire came home bravely the second place tara may had picked it back up and tebow pinot has refound his mojo as well it bodes very well for the next stage and what if this man ramco ever nepal desperately wanted to finish in front of his nearest rivals henrik mass and primos roglic no time gaps between them were declared so the overall stays the same albeit other rivals falling away jay vine in charge of his own destiny and so but surely moving up the general classification as well he won today by 43 seconds ahead of solaire and tara may tivo pino 47 seconds in arrears ever nepal then led home mass roglic with yates rodriguez and sebastian reichenbach rounding out the top ten a real battle it was forever nepal he'll have to do it all again on stage nine evan paul leads this race by that margin of 28 seconds over mass roglic is there so is rodriguez and ayuso and so we race on with jay vine in a polka dot jersey two stage wins to his name and what more lies at the feet of batman max pedersen equally delighted on this day taking control of the green jersey after a magnificent performance in the break and snaffling those sprint points this is what lays ahead before the rest day who will be able to go all in when you look at the parkour you indeed wonder it is going to be brutal la welter never disappoints but this weekend could prove pivotal do not miss stage nine in the run to the rest day
